steps: Go to https://sandbox.braintreegateway.com and sign in (or create a free sandbox account at https://www.braintreegateway.com/sandbox)
Once logged in, navigate to Settings (gear icon) in the top right
Your Merchant ID is displayed at the top of the API keys page
Under 'API Keys', click 'Generate New API Key'
Copy the Public Key shown in the table, then click 'View' next to it to reveal the Private Key
Save all three credentials: Merchant ID, Public Key, and Private Key
For production, log into https://www.braintreegateway.com and repeat the same steps

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key (Base64 public_key:private_key)
credential_fields.description: Braintree uses HTTP Basic Auth. Encode 'public_key:private_key' in base64 and pass as the Authorization header with 'Basic ' prefix. You also need the Merchant ID for the GraphQL endpoint.

notes: Braintree provides a free sandbox environment for testing — no real money is processed
All API operations go through a single GraphQL endpoint at /graphql
Authentication uses HTTP Basic Auth: base64-encode 'public_key:private_key' and set as Authorization header with 'Basic ' prefix
The Braintree-Version header is required for all GraphQL requests (e.g., '2019-01-01')
Each gateway user has their own set of API keys; do not share private keys
Production credentials are separate from sandbox credentials
